seed: longing vs resolve; repair_vs_damage; mirror_broken; pulse; barcode against broken_dots; lit_edges; contained_vs_overflowing; thread; serenity_urgency | abstract variation request 88; target batch count 500
title: Tension of Growth
reasoning: The emotional tone of longing versus resolve creates a beautiful tension marked by a dynamic interplay of colors. I selected a palette of ripe green, acid green, honey gold, deep olive, and cream to reflect the nuances of desire and fulfillment—vivid greens suggest growth and urgency, while creamy tones offer a calming balance. The left side embodies the longing with vibrant, flame-like patterns leading up to broken pixels that signify interruption. The right side transitions into the resolve, with channel-like formations of muted tones representing steadfastness. The main collision occurs centrally where the two patterns collide, making a statement about the push and pull of fleeting aspirations and firm foundations. This differentiation highlights the emotional complexity within the square, steering clear of representational imagery to focus on the abstract essence of the narrative.

layout.name: split_vertical
layout.description: Left 4 columns are emotion A, right 4 columns are emotion B.

composition.name: near_vs_far
composition.idea: Close paired marks are separated from distant echoes by a wide quiet field.

referenceExample.title: Mourning Opens
referenceExample.pattern: dark veil splits where green and pale yellow marks climb through

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
